quote: 1. Cutting losses quickly is one of the most important rules of stock trading. Even the best traders are wrong a lot of the time.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 2. It's not whether you're right or wrong, but how much money you make when you're right and how much you lose when you're wrong.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 3. Trade what you see, not what you think.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 4. It takes courage to be a pig.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 5. Risk management is the most important thing to be well understood. It's a way to ensure ongoing survival.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 6. The whole secret to winning big in the stock market is not how much you make on your winners, but how much you don't lose on your losers.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 7. I look for stocks making new highs, not new lows.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 8. I am much more interested in 'when' than 'why'. I don't need to know why a stock should go up; all I need to know is whether it is likely to go up.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 9. The big money is made by sitting and being patient, not by trading every day.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 10. Never, ever invest in the present. It doesn't matter what a company's earnings are, what they have been or what they will be. You have to visualize the situation 18 months from now, and whatever that is, that's where the price will be, not where it is today.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 11. Earnings don't move the overall market; it's the Federal Reserve Board... focus on the central banks, and focus on the movement of liquidity. It's liquidity that moves markets.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 12. To have lasting success in the stock market, you must decide once and for all that it's more important to make money than to be right. Your ego must take a backseat.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 13. The way to build superior long-term returns is through preservation of capital and home runs.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 14. Buy what you know — look at the brands around you, look at the items you use, the clothes you wear.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 15. What I needed to do was to focus on stocks that strictly fit the criteria.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 16. I'd rather be out of a stock wishing I was in, than in a stock wishing I was out.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 17. You must define your risk before you enter a trade, and you must never violate that risk level once it's set.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 18. I always cut my losses at 7 to 8 percent, no matter how good the story sounds.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 19. Not losing big is the single most important factor for winning big. As a speculator, losing is not a choice, but how much you lose is.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 20. I never use valuation to time the market. I use liquidity considerations and technical analysis for timing.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 21. If you want to reap big gains in the market, make up your mind right now that you are going to separate trading from your ego. It's more important to make money than it is to be right.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 22. Soros has taught me that when you have tremendous conviction on a trade, you have to go for the jugular.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 23. As far as Soros is concerned, when you're right on something, you can't own enough.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 24. The elements of good trading are: (1) cutting losses, (2) cutting losses, and (3) cutting losses.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 25. The more disciplined you can get, the better you are going to do in the market. The more you listen to tips and rumors, the more money you're likely to lose.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 26. Stocks should be at a profit the first day you buy them. In fact, having a profit on the first day is one of the best indicators that you are going to make money on the trade.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 27. The greatest investors make large concentrated bets where they have a lot of conviction. They're not buying 35 or 40 names and diversifying.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 28. I believe that good investors are successful not because of their IQ, but because they have an investing discipline.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 29. Long-term success in the stock market has nothing to do with hope or luck. Winning stock traders have rules and a well-thought-out plan. Conversely, losers lack rules, or if they have rules, they don't stick to them for very long; they deviate.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 30. You make your best decisions when they're your own decisions, and you have no one else to blame.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 31. The first thing I heard when I got in the business was bulls make money, bears make money, and pigs get slaughtered. I'm here to tell you I was a pig. And I strongly believe the only way to make long-term returns in our business that are superior is by being a pig.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 32. My goal is not to buy at the lowest or cheapest price but at the "right" price, just as a stock is ready to move significantly higher. Trying to pick a bottom is unnecessary and a waste of time; it misses the whole point.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 33. You must have a love for the market in order to be successful in this game. Really, a huge interest.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 34. When you truly commit to something, you have no alternative but success.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 35. Virtually every superperformance phase in big, winning stocks occurred while the stock price was in a definite price uptrend. In almost every case, the trend was identifiable early in the superperformance advance.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 36. Don't ever feel that you are very good. The second you do, you are dead.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 37. If you're early on in your career and they give you a choice between a great mentor or higher pay, take the mentor every time. It's not even close. And don't even think about leaving that mentor until your learning curve peaks.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 38. Those who trade without a stop eventually stop trading.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 39. The maximum loss I allow is 7 percent, and usually I am out of a losing stock a lot quicker.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 40. I never try to make money in the stock market. I buy on the assumption that they could close the market the next day and not reopen it for five years.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 41. Trading without a stop-loss is like driving a car without brakes.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 42. To compound your money, and not your mistakes, your goal is to buy on the way up—not on the way down.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 43. Concentrating your bets decreases your overall risk because where you tend to be in trouble is if you have 35 or 40 names. If you have a big massive position, it has your attention.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 44. Investing styles may differ among successful market players, but without exception, winning stock traders share certain key traits required for success. Fall short in those qualities and you will surely part ways with your money.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 45. The single most important advice I can give anybody is: Learn from your mistakes. That is the only way to become a successful trader.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 46. I like putting all my eggs in one basket and then watching the basket very carefully.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 47. Calculating risks shrewdly is the main ingredient for consistent superior performance. Pros play percentage ball, and that's why, in the long run, they are more consistent than amateurs.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 48. Every time I buy a stock, I write down the reasons why I bought it. Doing this helps cement in my mind the characteristics of a winning stock. Maybe even more important, it helps me learn from my mistakes.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 49. When you sell half, if the stock goes higher you say to yourself, 'Thank goodness I kept half.' If the stock goes lower, you'll say, 'Thank goodness I sold half.' Psychologically, it's a win/win either way.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 50. You need to be decisive, open-minded, flexible and competitive.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 51. I put a lot more weight into how this stock is acting relative to the S&P — you can see real divergences when stocks are making new highs and the relative strength line is not.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 52. Don't bet big unless a) you love your thesis, and b) you're hot.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 53. There really is no intelligent reason to increase your trading size if your positions are showing losses.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 54. The higher the relative strength the better. Once the relative strength starts falling off, I usually get out of the stock.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 55. I think contrarianism is overrated. Soros used to say the crowd's right 80% of the time. You just can't be caught in the other 20% because you can get your head handed to you. I get some intellectual satisfaction out of playing in the 20%, but as a concept, I think contrarianism is overrated.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 56. Ideally, I like to concentrate my capital in the best names, for example, devoting 20 to 25 percent of my portfolio in each of my top four or five stock picks.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 57. If I get stopped out, I get stopped out. I don't think, I just get out.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 58. When you tell yourself, I'm going to break my rule just this once, you open the door to losing discipline, because it's never just 'one time.'
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 59. I think a lot of my performance has been because I'm flexible in terms of the assets I use. I'm not afraid to just play in bonds or currencies or this or that.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 60. All the stocks we buy have some institutional sponsorship because mutual funds have such buying power that when they really like a company, they keep on buying it, and it keeps on going.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 61. I studied historical models of great winning stocks to ingrain in my mind what a stock looked like before it made a major move. I tried to get to the point where I was looking at the exact same things O'Neil did. He was my role model.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 62. You can get so focused on the results that you don't do what you should be doing.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 63. I always try to keep losses very small. 7 per cent is a great percentage to use.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 64. In the stock market, you can make money or you can make excuses, but you can't make both. Do whatever it takes to eliminate your own personal excuses. Your road to success starts by taking responsibility.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 65. Never stray from the basics of rule number one of always going in with a plan.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 66. The market keeps teaching the same lesson to every serious practitioner who is willing to listen.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 67. One of the reasons of my success was open mindedness to various asset classes. It gives you the discipline to not play when you shouldn't be playing.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 68. You must not risk more than you can reasonably expect to gain; otherwise, you're stacking the odds against you, and that's gambling.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 69. I went from mediocre to a stellar performer when I told myself: To heck with worrying about the money and obsessing over the scoreboard. I'm just going to focus on being the best trader I can be and sticking to the rules. Then the money followed.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 70. If you try to learn from every single trade that you make, you are only going to get better and better as time goes on.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 71. If you find yourself getting stopped out of your positions over and over, there can only be two things wrong: 1. Your stock selection criteria are flawed. 2. The general market environment is hostile.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 72. It's not enough to have knowledge, a dream, or passion; it's what you do with what you know that counts. The best time to get started is now!
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 73. I've also never hung onto a security if the reason I bought it has changed.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 74. What you have to look at is what people think it's going to earn. If you can see something in two years is going to be entirely different than the conventional wisdom, that's how you make money.
speaker: Stanley Druckenmiller

quote: 75. My goal is just to become a better and better trader, more and more disciplined, so I can be more and more successful in the market. I am disciplined in implementing the system and sticking to it.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 76. With every trade I make, my approach is always 'risk-first.' By thinking and acting 'risk-first,' you know exactly what you stand to lose if you're wrong, which in trading is far more important than what you stand to gain if you're right.
speaker: Mark Minervini

quote: 77. If you want to make money in the market, if you want to be successful in anything, you really got to put in a lot of time.
speaker: David Ryan

quote: 78. My longevity stems from the fact that I try to control risk as much as possible. When the market starts down I sell quickly and don't let the losses get out of hand.
speaker: David Ryan
